mission.title: submariner: Add 1 HA Failure Scenario to E2E testing (POC)
mission.description: Add 1 HA Failure Scenario to E2E testing (POC). Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current submariner deployment
steps.description:
Verify your submariner version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner
helm list -n submariner 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working submariner installation.

steps.title: Review submariner configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant submariner configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner
kubectl get configmap -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=submariner
```
Purpose of this issue : 
To automate - add to e2e (GO) a single HA failure test from the scenarios here : 
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11MV7CZmt13D8D4WYJTaddzQxSrXFRrn3UG0LiQKFVwY/edit#gid=0

More specifically :
scenario no.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add 1 HA Failure Scenario to E2E testing (POC)
steps.description:
Yes, Scenario-1 and Scenario-7 are kind of covered in the current e2e redundancy tests where the connectivity is validated (not downtime). Also, I agree with what  mentioned. The amount of downtime largely depends on the environment as there are many factors involved that can impact the behavior. So such tests IMHO are more suitable as subctl tests (not as e2e). 

Well, we don't have to try each and every combination of Pod location. The e2e redundancy tests try out the following combinations

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner
kubectl get events -n submariner --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add 1 HA Failure Scenario to E2E testing (POC)" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Yes, Scenario-1 and Scenario-7 are kind of covered in the current e2e redundancy tests where the connectivity is validated (not downtime). Also, I agree with what  mentioned. The amount of downtime largely depends on the environment as there are many factors involved that can impact the behavior. So such tests IMHO are more suitable as subctl tests (not as e2e).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with submariner installed or the issue environment reproducible.
